serving.natural_language_portion: One standard crispy cream puff (chocolate) weighs approximately 35 g and is typically consumed as a single-piece snack or dessert item.

nutrient_source.title: Crispy Cream Puff (Chocolate) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Crispy Cream Puff (Chocolate) X 1Pc (cr_id G00011022). The per-100g profile shows unusually low energy (42 kcal/100g) and near-zero protein and fat, which is atypical for a choux-based cream puff. This may reflect the product as a very light, aerated confection or a data anomaly. Values are preserved as reported by HPB; users should verify against product packaging. Protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB source for this specific product entry. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source.

gi_source.title: Choux pastry / cream puff analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database; refined wheat flour pastry reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific HPB-listed crispy cream puff product. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the closest analogous food class in the University of Sydney GI Database: refined wheat flour choux pastry with sugar-based cream filling. Refined pastry shells have GI values in the 65–75 range; chocolate cream filling adds sugar with negligible fibre, supporting a high-GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (70) × carbohydrate per serving (10.36 g/100g × 35 g serving = 3.626 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 2.54, rounded to 2.56 using standard GL formula. This is a low GL per single piece due to small serving size, but the high GI warrants caution for glycaemic-sensitive individuals.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100g) with zero dietary fibre and negligible potassium (5 mg/100g); added sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this product due to its high sugar-to-fibre ratio and negligible potassium; added sugar intake above 10% of daily energy is associated with elevated blood pressure per AHA 2018 guidelines, and this product provides no compensatory potassium or fibre to offset that risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~70) refined pastry with 9.94 g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre, resulting in rapid postprandial glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this product because the combination of high-GI refined choux pastry, high sugar content, and zero dietary fibre produces rapid postprandial glucose elevation with no fibre-mediated attenuation of glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Refined sugar (9.94 g/100g), likely saturated fat from chocolate cream filling, zero fibre, and negligible micronutrient density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit this product as its high refined sugar content, probable saturated fat from the chocolate cream filling, and absence of dietary fibre and cardioprotective micronutrients are inconsistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns recommended by the American Heart Association.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined sugar and likely saturated fat from chocolate cream filling are pro-inflammatory; zero fibre provides no gut microbiome benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit this product as refined sugar and saturated fat from the chocolate cream filling may promote systemic inflammation, and the absence of dietary fibre, omega-3 fatty acids, and antioxidants provides no anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crispy Cream Puff (Chocolate) is a single-serve baked choux pastry dessert popular in Singapore, consisting of a light, crispy hollow shell made from choux dough (wheat flour, butter, eggs, water) filled with a chocolate-flavoured cream. The shell is baked until puffed and golden, then filled with a sweetened chocolate cream or custard. The texture contrasts a thin, crisp outer shell with a soft, rich chocolate cream interior. Per the HPB Singapore Foodo database, this product has an unusually low energy density of 42 kcal per 100g with near-zero protein (0g) and very low fat (0.25g), which is atypical for traditional cream puffs and may reflect a highly aerated or reduced-fat commercial formulation; users should verify against product packaging. Carbohydrate content is 10.36 g/100g, predominantly sugar (9.94 g/100g), with zero dietary fibre. Sodium is low at 3 mg/100g. The high sugar content combined with zero fibre and a high estimated glycaemic index (~70) makes this product unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes or those managing blood glucose. The refined sugar and likely saturated fat from the chocolate cream filling are also concerns for cardiovascular disease and hypertension management. This product is vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. It is commonly consumed as a snack or dessert in Singapore and is sold as a single-piece portion of approximately 35g.
common_user_queries: Calories in chocolate cream puff Singapore
Is cream puff suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011022, confidence 0.95 per source record). The HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat flour choux pastry, clearly documented as an estimate. Multilingual names are provided for all mandatory Singaporean cuisine languages.
notes: 1. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database entry G00011022 for this specific commercial product formulation. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — this product contains no fibre by formulation. Magnesium_mg is reported as 0.0 per HPB source (confirmed zero / trace below detection threshold). 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (-1): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid breakdown, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific HPB product entry are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. 3. NUTRITIONAL ANOMALY: The per-100g profile of 42 kcal, 0g protein, 0.25g fat, and 10.36g carbohydrate is atypical for a traditional cream puff (which typically contains 250–350 kcal/100g with significant fat and protein). This may reflect a highly aerated, reduced-fat commercial product or a data entry anomaly in the HPB source. Values are preserved as reported; downstream users should verify against product packaging. 4. GI ESTIMATE: GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour choux pastry from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement exists for this specific product. 5. HALAL STATUS: Not confirmed as halal-certified; flag set to false pending verification. 6. SERVING SIZE: 35g estimated for a single cream puff piece; actual product weight should be verified from packaging.
